Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) plus Docetaxel (75 mg/m² for 21 days, up to nine cycles). ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors.

Trial Name.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer: Impact of Metastatic Burden and Long-term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 Trial

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS)

Treatment Class.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel; Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Alone

Type of Therapy.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) plus Docetaxel (D) or ADT alone

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.value: The overall risk judgment for the evidence presented in this randomized controlled trial, especially concerning the non-significant survival improvements in subgroups, indicates a high risk of bias or low certainty. This is attributed to several methodological issues, including the retrospective analysis of metastatic extent, insufficient statistical power for subgroup analyses (being 'clearly underpowered'), the study not being initially designed for such post-hoc analyses, and strong departures from proportional hazards assumptions.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.value: For biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS): Median PFS was 22.9 months (95% CI, 19.5-28.4). The hazard ratio for the comparison of ADT plus D vs ADT was 0.67 (95% CI, 0.54–0.84; p < 0.001). For radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS): Median PFS was 22.9 months (95% CI, 20.5-31.4). The hazard ratio for the comparison of ADT plus D vs ADT was 0.69 (95% CI, 0.55–0.87; p = 0.002).

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.value: For biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS): Median PFS was 12.9 months (95% CI, 11.9–17.7). The hazard ratio for the comparison of ADT plus D vs ADT was 0.67 (95% CI, 0.54–0.84; p < 0.001). For radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS): Median PFS was 15.3 months (95% CI, 12.4-19.8). The hazard ratio for the comparison of ADT plus D vs ADT was 0.69 (95% CI, 0.55–0.87; p = 0.002).
